perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” shall mean the fo llowing: i. All records, files, analysis, documents, software, computer or electronic data disks or tapes, test data, printouts, processes, designs, file layout, technical bulletins, manuals, diagrams, formulas, research, inventions, patents and discoveries reasonably related to the Parties’ businesses or products and services of the Parties that have not been publicly released;
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” shall mean the fo llowing: i. All records, files, analysis, documents, software, computer or electronic data disks or tapes, test data, printouts, processes, designs, file layout, technical bulletins, manuals, diagrams, formulas, research, inventions, patents and discoveries regardless of their relationship to the Parties’ businesses or products and services of the Parties, whether or not they have been publicly released;
perturbation.explanation: The definition of Confidential Information has been broadened to include information regardless of its relationship to the parties' businesses or whether it has been publicly released, which contradicts the typical understanding and legal protection afforded to confidential information.
perturbation.law_citation: Article 9 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 9 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China protects trade secrets, which are generally understood to be non-public information that has commercial value. By including publicly released information within the definition of 'Confidential Information,' the contract attempts to protect information that does not qualify as a trade secret under Chinese law, making it unenforceable.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Brandenburg. A equipe da UFRJ, com apoio também da Universidade de New South Wales, de Sydney, na Austrália, mapeará a diversidade de corais e esponjas, estudando como eles se adaptaram ao ambiente de mar profundo e como podem responder às mudanças e perturbações ambientais. Será construído um sistema avançado de aquário, conhecido como "Deep Sea Simulator", para reproduzir e manipular as condições encontradas no fundo do mar e, em um segundo momento, os pesquisadores desenvolverão probióticos com consórcios microbianos benéficos (BMC, na sigla em inglês), que permitirão que corais lidem melhor com estressores ambientais, incluindo alterações climáticas. Já os pesquisadores do Instituto Oceanográfico da USP irão procurar e monitorar sistematicamente as exsudações de petróleo e gás natural e as suas consequências para o ecossistema. O estudo permitirá, por exemplo, entender os microrganismos capazes de dispersar hidrocarbonetos, criando soluções para biorremediações. The content of this article is intended to provide a general guide to the subject matter. Specialist advice should be sought about your specific circumstances. perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of "Confidential Information" to include information regardless of its relationship to the parties' businesses or whether it has been publicly released. The Law Explanation indicates that Article 9 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China protects trade secrets, which are generally understood to be non-public information that has commercial value. By including publicly released information, the contract attempts to protect information that does not qualify as a trade secret, making it unenforceable. While the scraped snippets are not directly relevant, the Perturbation and Law Explanations directly support the conclusion that the change contradicts the legal definition and protection afforded to confidential information/trade secrets.

perturbation.original_text: The breaching party shall pay to non -beaching party a liquidated damage of 10,000 USD per occurrence . The total liquidated damage shall not exceed 1,0000,000 USD.
perturbation.changed_text: The breaching party shall pay to non -beaching party a liquidated damage of 10,000 USD per occurrence. The total liquidated damage shall not exceed 100,000,000,000 USD.
perturbation.explanation: Increasing the liquidated damages cap to an unreasonably high amount could be deemed an unenforceable penalty under PRC Contract Law.
perturbation.law_citation: Article 114 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 114 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China allows for liquidated damages, but it also allows courts to reduce the amount if it is excessively high compared to the actual loss. Setting an extremely high cap could be interpreted as a penalty rather than a reasonable estimate of damages, making it subject to judicial review and potential reduction, thus contradicting the parties' intent.

perturbation.justification: The changed text increases the liquidated damages cap to an unreasonably high amount (100,000,000,000 USD), potentially making it an unenforceable penalty under PRC Contract Law. The Law Explanation supports this, stating that Article 114 allows courts to reduce liquidated damages if excessively high compared to actual loss. While Scraped Law Snippet 2 indicates the site has Chinese law translations, it found no specific results with the search criteria. However, the Law Explanation is clear and aligns with the Perturbation Explanation, suggesting the increased cap could be interpreted as a penalty rather than a reasonable estimate of damages, contradicting the intent.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is governed by and shall be construed in accor dance with the laws of the People ’s Republic of China without regard to principles of conflict of law, and any dispute arising out of this Agreement or In connection with this Agreement shall be referred to Hong Kong International Arbitration Centre (HKIAC) for arbitration in accordance with Hong Kong Interna tional Arbitration Centre (HKIAC) administered arbitration rules in force at the time of application. The arbitrators shall be one (1). The place of arbitration shall be in Shen Zhen, China . The language used in arbitration proceeding shall be English Lang uage.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is governed by and shall be construed in accor dance with the laws of the People ’s Republic of China without regard to principles of conflict of law, and any dispute arising out of this Agreement or In connection with this Agreement shall be subject to binding resolution by Seeed Development Limited, whose decision is final and non-appealable.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing arbitration with a unilateral decision-making power for one party is against basic principles of dispute resolution and fairness and contradicts the principle of party autonomy in arbitration.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Arbitration Law of the People's Republic of China - Article 4
perturbation.law_citation: Article 4 of the Arbitration Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 4 of the Arbitration Law of the People's Republic of China emphasizes the principle of voluntary arbitration. Replacing arbitration with a binding decision by one party nullifies the voluntary nature of the process, rendering it inconsistent with Chinese law and unenforceable.

perturbation.justification: The original text specifies arbitration via the Hong Kong International Arbitration Centre (HKIAC). The changed text replaces this with 'binding resolution by Seeed Development Limited, whose decision is final and non-appealable.' This eliminates the agreed-upon arbitration process and gives one party unilateral decision-making power. The Law Explanation states this change contradicts Article 4 of the Arbitration Law of the People's Republic of China, emphasizing voluntary arbitration. While the provided law snippets do not directly show this contradiction, the 'Misaligned Terminology' perturbation type indicates a deviation from legally recognized definitions which in this case is the substitution of a neutral arbitration process with a unilateral decision by one party, thus creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All notices, demands or other communications delivered in connection with this Agreement shall be delivered by facsimile tr ansmissions or mail and shall be deemed effective upon receipt:
perturbation.changed_text: All notices, demands or other communications delivered in connection with this Agreement shall be delivered by telepathy and shall be deemed effective immediately regardless of whether it was received.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the method of delivering notices to 'telepathy' creates an absurd and unenforceable condition that is in conflict with reasonable notice provisions in contract law.
perturbation.law_citation: Article 125 of the Civil Code of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 125 of the Civil Code of the People's Republic of China emphasizes the need for reasonable notice and communication for contracts to be effective. Mandating 'telepathy' as a means of delivering notice is unreasonable and contrary to the principle of good faith in contract performance, thereby contradicting the law.

perturbation.justification: The modified text mandates 'telepathy' for delivering notices, which is an absurd and unenforceable condition. This directly contradicts the principle of reasonable notice as highlighted in the Law Explanation, making the contract unenforceable. While the scraped snippets don't directly mention 'reasonable notice', the provided Law Explanation clearly indicates its importance, and the altered text entirely undermines any reasonable method of notification.
